lines.speaker: Easter Bunny
lines.text: Oh no, why today of all days?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What's wrong Mr Rabbit?

lines.speaker: Easter Bunny
lines.text: I'm having an eggceptionally bad day. But if you must know, my Egg making machine is shell dry and the next shipment of fuel will not be here for a while!

lines.speaker: Easter Bunny
lines.text: I would love it if a cracking adventurer, such as yourself, would help a Bunny in need.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I would love to help you mix up some more fuel.

lines.speaker: Easter Bunny
lines.text: Thanks for eggreeing to help me.

lines.speaker: Easter Bunny
lines.text: There are three ingredients that are needed to make the fuel.

lines.speaker: Easter Bunny
lines.text: The first ingredient is Dried cocoa. During any other time of year this ingredient would be eggceptionally hard to get.

lines.speaker: Easter Bunny
lines.text: But if I'm spot on with my information, there should be a merchant rolling into Al Kharid soon. He should sell you some.

lines.speaker: Easter Bunny
lines.text: Try not to bring me camel dung, they do look similar.

lines.speaker: Easter Bunny
lines.text: The second ingredient is really high quality milk.

lines.speaker: Easter Bunny
lines.text: The most eggceptional milk around has got to be milk from Hosidius, I'm sure Duke Horacio's cook can help you out.

lines.speaker: Easter Bunny
lines.text: The third ingredient is some cracking spices, very special ones. Can't go into detail, trade secret and all.

lines.speaker: Easter Bunny
lines.text: I'm sure if you talk to Martin in Draynor, he'll be a good egg and help you out if you ask for Special spices.

lines.speaker: Easter Bunny
lines.text: Since you're doing errands for me. Feel Free to use my tunnels to get around.

lines.speaker: Easter Bunny
lines.text: But don't make a yolk out of it, you may only use each tunnel once. Can't have you interrupting my workers any more than that.

lines.speaker: Easter Bunny
lines.text: Also, if you ever forget what you need, there will be a worker nearby who can remind you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll get right on it. Be back soon.

lines.speaker: Easter Bunny
lines.text: See you soon!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Get stuffed.

lines.speaker: Easter Bunny
lines.text: I'll have you know this is no yolking matter.

sections.title: After starting the event, before the supplies are gathered

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where do I get the Dried cocoa?

lines.speaker: Easter Bunny
lines.text: During any other time of year this ingredient would be a nightmare to get.

lines.speaker: Easter Bunny
lines.text: But if I'm spot on with my information, there should bea merchant making his way from the Kharidian Desert to Al Kharid.

lines.speaker: Easter Bunny
lines.text: I'd suggest checking the Shantay Pass.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where do I get the bottle of Hosidius milk?

lines.speaker: Easter Bunny
lines.text: The most eggceptional milk around has got to be milk from Hosidius, I'm sure Duke Horacio's cook in Lumbridge castle can help you out.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where do I get the Special spices?

lines.speaker: Easter Bunny
lines.text: I'm sure if you talk to Martin in Draynor, he'll be a good egg and help you out if you ask for Special spices.

lines.speaker: Easter Bunny
lines.text: He should get the hint.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Goodbye.

lines.speaker: Easter Bunny
lines.text: Keep on rolling my friend!

sections.title: When first spoken to, after starting the quest

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Easter Bunny! I knew I would find you here.

lines.speaker: Easter Bunny
lines.text: Easter Bunny... Now there's a name I haven't heard in a long time...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How long exactly?

lines.speaker: Easter Bunny
lines.text: Oh, almost a year.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What have people been calling you instead?

lines.speaker: Easter Bunny
lines.text: I go by many names.

lines.speaker: Easter Bunny
lines.text: Some call me 'Eggrabbit'.

lines.speaker: Easter Bunny
lines.text: Others call me 'Long Ears Johnson'.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Mhmm?

lines.speaker: Easter Bunny
lines.text: Many times have I walked past Varrock West Bank and been called 'Sellin lobbies 200gp'.

lines.speaker: Easter Bunny
lines.text: I don't really understand that one.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you think perhaps they weren't talking to you?

lines.speaker: Easter Bunny
lines.text: Who could blame them? I'm a failure!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh come on now, what do you mean?

lines.speaker: Easter Bunny
lines.text: You call me the Easter Bunny? What right do I have to that name?

lines.speaker: Easter Bunny
lines.text: I don't feel the spirit of Easter within me.

lines.speaker: Easter Bunny
lines.text: *sniff*

lines.speaker: Easter Bunny
lines.text: I'm just a big dumb rabbit!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Aww, that's... come on!

lines.speaker: Easter Bunny
lines.text: I've ruined Easter for everyone!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Aww...

lines.speaker: Easter Bunny
lines.text: WAHHHH!

sections.title: When spoken to a second time

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello Easter Bunny, how are you feeling?

lines.speaker: Easter Bunny
lines.text: Maybe I'll make my way through this sorry old world as a clown...

sections.title: Cheering up Easter Bunny with Bowley Moley's Bowling

lines.speaker: Bowley Moley
lines.text: Hello! Have you come to take part in the festivities?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The Easter Bunny is looking awfully glum this year.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Perhaps we can help raise the Easter spirits?

lines.speaker: Bowley Moley
lines.text: You want to do some necromancy... To cheer up the Easter Bunny?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No no no! It's just a figure of speech.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I just wonder if we could do anything to help.

lines.speaker: Bowley Moley
lines.text: Leave it to me boss.

lines.speaker: Bowley Moley
lines.text: I happen to know the E.B. is a big fan of bowling. In fact, he used to be a wizard on the lanes. A champion!

lines.speaker: Bowley Moley
lines.text: I also happen to have the ability to...

lines.speaker: Bowley Moley
lines.text: ...Rig the game in his favour.

lines.speaker: Bowley Moley
lines.text: Which is handy, he's very out of practise nowadays.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Won't he suspect something?

lines.speaker: Bowley Moley
lines.text: Oh no! You see, I have these gears set up just here.

lines.speaker: Bowley Moley
lines.text: Wait, are you just trying to get my trade secrets?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: This is strictly a humanitarian effort!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Bun-itarian..?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Look, I just want to get Easter back on track.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You can keep your hustle going, I just want to help the Easter Bunny.

lines.speaker: Bowley Moley
lines.text: Oh alright, since I do owe the big softie. But I've got my whiskers on you!

lines.speaker: Easter Bunny
lines.text: So, what is it you wanted to show me, [player name]?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, Bowley here let me in on your secret. Bowling champion three years in a row.

lines.speaker: Easter Bunny
lines.text: Oh. Those were the days, I haven't partaken for while now though...

lines.speaker: Easter Bunny
lines.text: I just haven't had the spring in my step with my egg responsibilities.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, here's your chance!

lines.speaker: Easter Bunny
lines.text: What do you mean? It's hardly fun without decent competition.

lines.speaker: Easter Bunny
lines.text: And nobody here quite likes bowling.

lines.speaker: Bowley Moley
lines.text: They what now?

lines.speaker: Bowley Moley
lines.text: I've only just gone and spent ages digging out this place and installing the machinery!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Nothing to worry about I'm sure!

lines.speaker: Easter Bunny
lines.text: Ah Bowley, it is good to see you again.

lines.speaker: Bowley Moley
lines.text: Long Ears Johnson. Come to terrorise me pins again?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: We'll see about that.

lines.speaker: Easter Bunny
lines.text: Oh yes, [player name] is apparently quite good.

lines.speaker: Easter Bunny
lines.text: It's been so long since I've had a decent set with a worthy opponent.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Chin up bucko, I'm here and ready to challenge your rule of these alleys!

lines.speaker: Easter Bunny
lines.text: Well, I suppose I'm not doing anything else. Why not?

lines.speaker: Easter Bunny
lines.text: A fine shot, but not quite enough!

lines.speaker: Easter Bunny
lines.text: Watch this!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: A lucky strike?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'd better shape up or this will be quite one sided.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And that would be no fun at all.

lines.speaker: Easter Bunny
lines.text: Ah, so you have some fight in you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I could do this all day.

lines.speaker: Easter Bunny
lines.text: That's exactly what I wanted to hear!

lines.speaker: Easter Bunny
lines.text: I'm surprised I hadn't heard about your prowess before, [player name].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ah you know me, the most modest adventurer in Gielinor.

lines.speaker: Easter Bunny
lines.text: Ah, well I suppose I should pack it in while I'm ahead.

lines.speaker: Easter Bunny
lines.text: But we should do this again.

lines.speaker: Bowley Moley
lines.text: Aye we should! Also if you could get the word out about my establishment, I'll be raking it in!

lines.speaker: Bowley Moley
lines.text: I mean... The Rabbit Hole will enjoy all the visitors?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, anyway...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It was a close match. How are you feeling now?

lines.speaker: Easter Bunny
lines.text: Oh it was fun but I think I'm just going to sit in the Rabbit Hole a bit longer.

lines.speaker: Easter Bunny
lines.text: I should be off. Always a pleasure, Bowley.

lines.speaker: Bowley Moley
lines.text: Aye, till next time.

sections.title: Talking to the Easter Bunny after bowling

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello Easter Bunny, how are you feeling?

lines.speaker: Easter Bunny
lines.text: I feel less horrible I suppose...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, that's a start!

sections.title: Using a fancy carrot on the Easter Bunny

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hey Bunny, would you like a fancy carrot?

lines.speaker: Easter Bunny
lines.text: Not really, I've eaten a lot of carrots recently.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: They're really good.

lines.speaker: Easter Bunny
lines.text: I'm alright, thank you though.

sections.title: Talking to the Easter Bunny while holding fancy carrot juice, or using fancy carrot juice on him

lines.speaker: Easter Bunny
lines.text: Oh, hello again, [player name].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello! I have a surprise for you.

lines.speaker: Easter Bunny
lines.text: Oh? A surprise? It's not something horrible I hope.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh no, it's something quite delicious actually.

lines.speaker: Easter Bunny
lines.text: Delicious?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: A lovely mug of carrot juice!

lines.speaker: Easter Bunny
lines.text: Oh please no more!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Don't worry, this is fancy carrot juice. I made it myself with some fantastic carrots.

lines.speaker: Easter Bunny
lines.text: You made it? Well in that case I suppose I might give it a try. As long as Wheeler hasn't been near it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I made sure of that. Go on!

lines.speaker: Easter Bunny
lines.text: Here we go then...

lines.speaker: Easter Bunny
lines.text: Oh...

lines.speaker: Easter Bunny
lines.text: Oh my...

lines.speaker: Easter Bunny
lines.text: Wow!

lines.speaker: Easter Bunny
lines.text: That was delicious, nothing like the usual slop.

lines.speaker: Easter Bunny
lines.text: Thank you, [player name], I really needed that.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You're welcome!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How are you feeling now? Any better?

lines.speaker: Easter Bunny
lines.text: Hmm... I'm feeling a bit more hopeful. Like spring is about to, well, spring! But I'm not sure if Easter is really my thing any more.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm sure I can help you remember how wonderful it is.

sections.title: Talking to the Easter Bunny after giving him the fancy carrot juice

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello Easter Bunny, how are you feeling?

lines.speaker: Easter Bunny
lines.text: Hmm... I'm feeling a bit more hopeful. Like spring is about to, well, spring! But I'm not sure if Easter is really my thing any more.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm sure I can help you remember how wonderful it is.

sections.title: Trying to talk to Easter Bunny with Easter buns without talking to Eggus Manus

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hmmm... I should check in with Eggus before I give one of these buns to the Easter Bunny. Quality control or something.

sections.title: Talking to the Easter Bunny after having Magus Manus test the bun

lines.speaker: Easter Bunny
lines.text: Hello...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hi! I'm back with an Easter treat, just for you.

lines.speaker: Easter Bunny
lines.text: Huh?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's something special.

lines.speaker: Easter Bunny
lines.text: Special eh? Why would you give me something special?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Because you deserve it.

lines.speaker: Easter Bunny
lines.text: I don't know if I deserve anything.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Of course you do!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Here, it's an Easter bun. Fresh out of the oven.

lines.speaker: Easter Bunny
lines.text: An Easter bun? I like those!

lines.speaker: Easter Bunny
lines.text: Yum! That was lovely. Thanks, [player name].

lines.speaker: Easter Bunny
lines.text: You're a good friend.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You are most welcome. Do you feel a bit more Easter- ey now?

lines.speaker: Easter Bunny
lines.text: Oh, I'm starting to remember now! Easter is great! But I just... Hmm, I don't know...

lines.speaker: Easter Bunny
lines.text: I feel like I need one more little treat before I do anything to help with Easter.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh for goodness sake...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Alright, let me think of something.

lines.speaker: Fry
lines.text: Excuse me, [player name], I believe I can help you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh?

lines.speaker: Fry
lines.text: Come here!

lines.speaker: Fry
lines.text: I couldn't help overhearing that you're trying to cheer up the Easter Bunny, and I have an idea.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well that's sort of nosey, but go on.

lines.speaker: Fry
lines.text: Let's put on a performance! that will cheer him up.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: A performance?

lines.speaker: Fry
lines.text: Yes! He's always enjoyed a good show.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well I suppose that could work...

lines.speaker: Fry
lines.text: I have some classic material we can try if that sounds appropriate?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't see why not.

lines.speaker: Fry
lines.text: Excellent! I've already talked to some of the other fellows in here about it too. They seem keen!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I wonder how Fry is getting on with that performance...

lines.speaker: Easter Bunny
lines.text: What was that?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, nothing. I just need to talk to Fry about something.

lines.speaker: Easter Bunny
lines.text: Oh, see you later then.

sections.title: Easter Bunny after cheered up two times

lines.speaker: Easter Bunny
lines.text: One more treat and I'll be ready to work on Easter again.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ANOTHER treat?

lines.speaker: Easter Bunny
lines.text: The last one!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It better be.

sections.title: Talking to the Easter Bunny after the performance

lines.speaker: Easter Bunny
lines.text: That dance was amazing! Well done. I feel so... Invigorated!

sections.title: Talking to the Easter Bunny after he mentions Varrock

lines.speaker: Easter Bunny
lines.text: Lawks! Look at the time, I should get back to Varrock to set up the Eggs!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: See you up there!

sections.title: Talking to the Easter Bunny near Varrock
Easter Bunny again after event is completed

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh it's always my pleasure to get these delicious eggs!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I mean... to help out a friend in need!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Isn't it strange something always happens around this time of year that needs me to come and save the day?

lines.speaker: Easter Bunny
lines.text: Oh really? I don't keep track, so much to do around now.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh well, I expect I'll be seeing you again next year.

lines.speaker: Easter Bunny
lines.text: Yes, till neggxt year!

sections.title: Easter Bunny again when inside the Rabbit Hole after event is completed

lines.speaker: Easter Bunny
lines.text: Would you please stop [player name], I'ts very hard to rush back here so fast.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You're coming back here, whenever I do?

lines.speaker: Easter Bunny
lines.text: Precisely, so we'd all appreciate it if you didn't.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But why?

lines.speaker: Easter Bunny
lines.text: Who knows what you'd get up to down here on your own.

lines.speaker: Easter Bunny
lines.text: I have something fun for you, [player name]!

lines.speaker: Easter Bunny
lines.text: Come back when you have more space.

lines.speaker: Easter Bunny
lines.text: Thanks for saving our bacon and eggs again, [player name]!
